heading: Covered misconduct in Coast Guard
label: 2533 Covered misconduct in Coast Guard

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Not later than March 1 each year, the Commandant shall submit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives a report on incidents of covered misconduct involving members of the Coast Guard, including recruits and officer candidates, and claims of retaliation related to the reporting of any such incident.

children.heading: .—Continuity of Data and Reporting
children.label: b .—Continuity of Data and Reporting
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Commandant shall ensure the continuity of data collection and reporting such that the ability to analyze trends is not compromised.

children.label: I
children.text: incidents of covered misconduct; and

children.label: II
children.text: incidents of retaliation against a member of the Coast Guard related to the reporting of covered misconduct, disaggregated by type of retaliation claim.

children.label: I
children.text: All incidents of covered misconduct and retaliation described in clause (i) reported to the Commandant or any other official of the Coast Guard during the preceding calendar year (referred to in this subsection as a “reported incident”).

children.label: II
children.text: The number of reported incidents committed against members of the Coast Guard.

children.label: III
children.text: The number of reported incidents committed by members of the Coast Guard.

children.label: IV
children.text: Information on reported incidents, in accordance with the policy prescribed under section 549G(b) of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2022 ( note), to the maximum extent practicable.10 U.S.C. 1561

children.label: V
children.text: The number of reported incidents that were entered into the Catch a Serial Offender system, including the number of such incidents that resulted in the identification of a potential or confirmed match.

children.label: VI
children.text: The number of reported incidents that were substantiated (referred to in this subsection as a “substantiated reported incident”).

children.label: aa
children.text: a brief description of the nature of the incident;

children.label: bb
children.text: whether the accused member has previously been convicted of sexual assault; and

children.label: cc
children.text: whether alcohol or other controlled or prohibited substances were involved in the incident, and a description of the involvement.

children.label: aa
children.text: conviction and sentence by court-martial, including charges and specifications for which convicted;

children.label: bb
children.text: acquittal of all charges at court-martial;

children.label: cc
children.text: as appropriate, imposition of a nonjudicial punishment under (article 15 of the Uniform Code of Military Justice);section 815 of title 10

children.label: dd
children.text: as appropriate, administrative action taken, including a description of each type of such action imposed;

children.label: ee
children.text: dismissal of all charges, including a description of each reason for dismissal and the stage at which dismissal occurred; and

children.label: ff
children.text: whether the accused member was administratively separated or, in the case of an officer, allowed to resign in lieu of court-martial, and the characterization (honorable, general, or other than honorable) of the service of the member upon separation or resignation.

children.label: aa
children.text: a narrative description of the retaliation claim;

children.label: bb
children.text: the nature of the relationship between the complainant and the individual accused of committing the retaliation; and

children.label: cc
children.text: the nature of the relationship between the individual accused of committing the covered misconduct and the individual accused of committing the retaliation.

children.label: X
children.text: The disposition of or action taken by the Coast Guard or any other Federal, State, local, or Tribal entity with respect to a substantiated reported incident.

children.label: aa
children.text: the status of the investigation or information relating to any referral to outside law enforcement entities;

children.label: bb
children.text: the official or office of the Coast Guard that received the complaint;

children.label: cc
children.text: a description of the results of such an investigation or information with respect to whether the results of the investigation were provided to the complainant; or

children.label: dd
children.text: whether the investigation substantiated an offense under chapter 47 of title 10 (the Uniform Code of Military Justice).

children.heading: .—Format
children.label: iii .—Format
children.text: With respect to the information and data required under clause (i), the Commandant shall report such information and data separately for each type of covered misconduct offense, and shall not aggregate the information and data for multiple types of covered misconduct offenses.

children.heading: .—Trends
children.label: B .—Trends
children.text: Subject to subsection (b), beginning on the date of enactment of the Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2025, each report required by subsection (a) shall include, for the preceding calendar year, an analysis or assessment of trends in the occurrence, as applicable, of incidents described in subparagraph (A)(i), since the date of enactment of the Coast Guard and Maritime Transportation Act of 2012 ().Public Law 112–213

children.heading: .—Response
children.label: C .—Response
children.text: Each report required under subsection (a) shall include, for the preceding calendar year, a description of the policies, procedures, processes, initiatives, investigations (including overarching investigations), research, or studies implemented by the Commandant in response to any incident described in subparagraph (A)(i) involving a member of the Coast Guard.

children.heading: .—Plan
children.label: D .—Plan
children.text: Each report required under subsection (a) shall include a plan for actions to be taken during the year following the year covered by the report to enhance the prevention of and response to incidents described in subparagraph (A)(i) involving members of the Coast Guard.

children.heading: .—Covered misconduct prevention and response activities
children.label: E .—Covered misconduct prevention and response activities
children.text: Each report required under subsection (a) shall include an assessment of the adequacy of covered misconduct prevention and response activities related to incidents described in subparagraph (A)(i) carried out by the Coast Guard during the preceding calendar year.

children.label: i
children.text: an analysis of the factors that may have contributed to such incidents;

children.label: ii
children.text: an assessment of the role of such factors in contributing to such incidents during such year; and

children.label: iii
children.text: recommendations for mechanisms to eliminate or reduce such contributing factors.

children.heading: Incidents involving recruits and officer candidates.—
children.label: 2 Incidents involving recruits and officer candidates.—
I
children.text: incidents of covered misconduct involving a recruit of the Coast Guard at Training Center Cape May or an officer candidate at the Coast Guard Officer Candidate School; and

children.label: II
children.text: incidents of retaliation against such a recruit or officer candidate related to the reporting of covered misconduct, disaggregated by type of retaliation claim.

children.label: aa
children.text: All incidents of covered misconduct and retaliation described in clause (i) reported to the Commandant or any other official of the Coast Guard during the preceding calendar year (referred to in this subsection as a “reported incident”).

children.label: bb
children.text: The number of reported incidents committed against recruits and officer candidates described in clause (i)(I).

children.label: cc
children.text: The number of reported incidents committed by such recruits and officer candidates.

children.label: dd
children.text: Information on reported incidents, in accordance with the policy prescribed under section 549G(b) of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2022 ( note), to the maximum extent practicable.10 U.S.C. 1561

children.label: AA
children.text: The number of reported incidents that were entered into the Catch a Serial Offender system.

children.label: BB
children.text: Of such reported incidents entered into such system, the number that resulted in the identification of a potential or confirmed match.

children.label: ff
children.text: The number of reported incidents that were substantiated (referred to in this subsection as a “substantiated reported incident”).

children.label: AA
children.text: a brief description of the nature of the incident; and

children.label: BB
children.text: whether alcohol or other controlled or prohibited substances were involved in the incident, and a description of the involvement.

children.label: AA
children.text: conviction and sentence by court-martial, including charges and specifications for which convicted;

children.label: BB
children.text: acquittal of all charges at court-martial;

children.label: CC
children.text: as appropriate, imposition of a nonjudicial punishment under (article 15 of the Uniform Code of Military Justice);section 815 of title 10

children.label: DD
children.text: as appropriate, administrative action taken, including a description of each type of such action imposed;

children.label: EE
children.text: dismissal of all charges, including a description of each reason for dismissal and the stage at which dismissal occurred; and

children.label: FF
children.text: whether the accused member was administratively separated or, in the case of an officer, allowed to resign in lieu of court-martial, and the characterization (honorable, general, or other than honorable) of the service of the member upon separation or resignation.

children.label: AA
children.text: a narrative description of the retaliation claim;

children.label: BB
children.text: the nature of the relationship between the complainant and the individual accused of committing the retaliation; and

children.label: CC
children.text: the nature of the relationship between the individual accused of committing the covered misconduct and the individual accused of committing the retaliation.

children.label: jj
children.text: The disposition of or action taken by the Coast Guard or any other Federal, State, local, or Tribal entity with respect to a substantiated reported incident.

children.label: AA
children.text: the status of the investigation or information relating to any referral to outside law enforcement entities;

children.label: BB
children.text: the official or office of the Coast Guard that received the complaint;

children.label: CC
children.text: a description of the results of such an investigation or information with respect to whether the results of the investigation were provided to the complainant; or

children.label: DD
children.text: whether the investigation substantiated an offense under chapter 47 of title 10 (the Uniform Code of Military Justice).

children.heading: .—Format
children.label: II .—Format
children.text: With respect to the information and data required under clause (i), the Commandant shall report such information and data separately for each type of covered misconduct offense, and shall not aggregate the information and data for multiple types of covered misconduct offenses.

children.heading: .—Trends
children.label: B .—Trends
children.text: Subject to subsection (b), beginning on the date of enactment of Coast Guard Authorization Act of 2025, each report required by subsection (a) shall include, for the preceding calendar year, an analysis or assessment of trends in the occurrence, as applicable, of incidents described in subparagraph (A)(i), since the date of enactment of the Coast Guard and Maritime Transportation Act of 2012 ().Public Law 112–213

children.label: i
children.text: a recruit of the Coast Guard at Training Center Cape May; or

children.label: ii
children.text: an officer candidate at the Coast Guard Officer Candidate School.

children.heading: .—Plan
children.label: D .—Plan
children.text: Each report required under subsection (a) shall include a written and detailed plan for actions to be taken during the year following the year covered by the report to enhance the prevention of and response to incidents described in subparagraph (A)(i) involving a recruit of the Coast Guard at Training Center Cape May or an officer candidate at the Coast Guard Officer Candidate School.

children.heading: .—Covered misconduct prevention and response activities
children.label: E .—Covered misconduct prevention and response activities
children.text: Each report required under subsection (a) shall include an assessment of the adequacy of covered misconduct prevention and response activities related to incidents described in subparagraph (A)(i) of this paragraph carried out by the Coast Guard during the preceding calendar year.

children.label: i
children.text: an analysis of the factors that may have contributed to such incidents;

children.label: ii
children.text: an assessment of the role of such factors in contributing to such incidents during such year; and

children.label: iii
children.text: recommendations for mechanisms to eliminate or reduce such contributing factors.

children.heading: .—Implementation status of accountability and transparency review directed actions
children.label: 3 .—Implementation status of accountability and transparency review directed actions
A
children.text: a description of actions taken to address each directed action during the year covered by the report;

children.label: B
children.text: the implementation status of each directed action;

children.label: i
children.text: a detailed action plan for implementation of the recommendation;

children.label: ii
children.text: an estimated timeline for implementation of the recommendation;

children.label: iii
children.text: description of changes the Commandant intends to make to associated Coast Guard policies so as to enable the implementation of the recommendation; and

children.label: iv
children.text: any other information the Commandant considers appropriate;

children.label: D
children.text: a description of the metrics and milestones used to measure completion, accountability, and effectiveness of each directed action;

children.label: E
children.text: a description of any additional actions the Commandant is taking to mitigate instances of covered misconduct within the Coast Guard;

children.label: F
children.text: any legislative change proposal necessary to implement the directed actions; and

children.label: G
children.text: a detailed list of funding necessary to implement the directed actions in a timely and effective manner, including a list of personnel needed for such implementation.

children.heading: .—Victim Confidentiality
children.label: d .—Victim Confidentiality
children.text: To the extent that information collected under the authority of this section is reported or otherwise made available to the public, such information shall be provided in a form that is consistent with applicable privacy protections under Federal law and does not jeopardize the confidentiality of victims.

children.heading: .—Substantiated Defined
children.label: e .—Substantiated Defined
children.text: In this section, the term “substantiated” has the meaning given the term under section 1631(c) of the Ike Skelton National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2011 ( note).10 U.S.C. 1561
